dsl_rule.translation_notes: Mapped short_window=10 to sma_10 and long_window=50 to sma_50. Entry requires both crossover detection and confirmation that short SMA is above long SMA. Exit triggers on bearish crossover. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT but structured as LONG entries with corresponding exits; SHORT signals would use inverse logic on the same crossover conditions.

backtest_rule.reason: Mapped short_window=10 to sma_10 and long_window=50 to sma_50. Entry requires both crossover detection and confirmation that short SMA is above long SMA. Exit triggers on bearish crossover. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT but structured as LONG entries with corresponding exits; SHORT signals would use inverse logic on the same crossover conditions.

source_rule_text: Generate a long entry signal when the short-term moving average crosses above the long-term moving average (bullish crossover), and generate an exit signal when the short-term moving average crosses below the long-term moving average (bearish crossover).
